author: daniellehrner
content: Yes, it looks like a Besu issue. I am looking into it

author: potuz
content: Besu's logs are crazy

author: potuz
content: Ok, so why are the three ELs failing only with Prysm though? what do we do that trigger this and no other CLs do?

author: barnabasbusa
content: prysm is special

author: tersec
content: I mean, if it's spec-valid does it matter?

author: potuz
content: I want to know because it may be different behavior, and I would want to know if the ELs are assuming certain behaviours

author: daniellehrner
content:
It seems Prysm is sending a lot of newPayload calls without calling forkchoiceUpdated. That is forcing Besu to keep a lot of intermediary states in memory and once we only have little memory left, this causes issues with other parts of the client.

Is it expected behavior to not send fcus after a newPayload? Other CLs seem not to do that

author: ahamlat
content: In besu, we trust only FCU to move the state head, so we apply the changes from previous newPayload requests once we receive FCU. A usual behaviour from besu perspective is newPayload -> Fcu -> newPayload -> Fcu ..

author: tersec
content: The spec does not require this

author: ahamlat
content: <@680337202521440470> can we review this behaviour together ? when following the chain, it makes sens to wait for FCU, but during catch up / sync, we can apply intermediate persist phases
